method_summary: The method uses a Gaussian-mixture-model-based preprocessing pipeline to extract segmented trajectories from noisy AIS data, then constructs a global knowledge graph with speed distributions stratified by vessel type, time of travel, and direction of travel for travel-time prediction.
key_innovation: Construction of a global knowledge graph with 5,433 geohash-3 nodes and 12,334 edges that enables travel-time predictions between any two locations via a hierarchical, priority-based system.
best_result: 69.1% of trajectories within 20% of actual arrival time on held-out test set
limitations_stated: Performance degrades on external test sets compared to held-out test sets
Method relies on AIS data, which may have gaps or inaccuracies
System requires a principled fallback for cases where historical statistics are insufficient
title: Historical Knowledge Graphs for Global Maritime Estimated Time of Arrival
